lessonSubtitleText: Test how well you know this story

questions.text: Why did Ruth and Naomi return to Israel?
answers: Their husbands had died
They wanted to go on a vacation
They were kicked out of their country
They were looking for treasure

questions.text: What does it mean to glean?
answers: To gather leftover grain from the field
To plant seeds on a farm
To cook food for others
To steal food from farmers

questions.text: Whose field did Ruth go to?
answers: Boaz
David
Saul
Samuel

questions.text: What does it mean that Boaz was a family redeemer?
answers: His job was to help and protect his family members
He ruled over many families
He collected taxes for the family
He was the strongest member of the family

questions.text: How was Boaz kind to Ruth?
answers: He gave her food, protected her, and told workers to leave extra grain for her
He gave her a house to live in
He gave her a gold ring
He wrote her a poem

questions.text: What did Naomi tell Ruth to do at night?
answers: Lie down near Boaz and uncover his feet
Sneak up and scare Boaz
Write a love poem to Boaz
Go to the food and steal extra grain

questions.text: What did Ruth ask Boaz to do, and what did he say?
answers: Marry her; Boaz said yes but needed to speak to a closer relative first
Give her money; Boaz said no
Give her more grain; Boaz agreed
Marry Naomi; he said, 'I guess so.'

questions.text: What was the name of Ruth and Boaz’s child?
answers: Obed
Jesse
David
Samuel

questions.text: Who was Ruth and Boaz’s great-grandson?
answers: King David
King Saul
Solomon
Moses

questions.text: What did Jesus do to redeem us?
answers: He paid the price of death for our sins
He married Ruth
He let us pick up grain from his field
He paid money to the devil to let us go free
